questions.question: Explain why concave mirrors can form both real and virtual images.
questions.answer: Concave mirrors can form real images when the object is placed beyond the focal point and virtual images when the object is placed between the focal point and the mirror.

questions.question: Why does a convex mirror always form a virtual image?
questions.answer: A convex mirror spreads reflected light rays outward, making them appear to originate from a point behind the mirror, forming a virtual image.

questions.question: Explain the importance of the law of reflection in image formation.
questions.answer: The law of reflection ensures that light rays reflect in a predictable manner, allowing mirrors to form clear images.

questions.question: Why are concave mirrors used in solar cookers?
questions.answer: Concave mirrors concentrate sunlight at a focal point, producing high temperatures useful for cooking.

questions.question: Explain why objects appear smaller in convex mirrors.
questions.answer: Convex mirrors spread light rays outward, forming diminished images behind the mirror.

questions.question: Why is a convex mirror preferred as a rear-view mirror in vehicles?
questions.answer: Convex mirrors provide a wider field of view, allowing drivers to see more area behind them.

questions.question: Explain how a convex lens forms a real image.
questions.answer: A convex lens converges light rays from an object so that they meet at a point on the other side of the lens, forming a real image.

questions.question: Why does a concave lens always produce a virtual image?
questions.answer: A concave lens diverges light rays, making them appear to come from a point behind the lens.

questions.question: Explain how lenses are used in cameras.
questions.answer: Camera lenses focus light onto film or sensors to produce clear images.

questions.question: Why do telescopes use convex lenses?
questions.answer: Convex lenses collect and focus light from distant objects, making them visible and magnified.

questions.question: Explain why microscopes use convex lenses.
questions.answer: Convex lenses magnify small objects by converging light rays to form enlarged images.

questions.question: Why do mirrors produce virtual images behind them?
questions.answer: Reflected rays diverge and appear to originate from behind the mirror, forming a virtual image.

questions.question: Explain why the image in a plane mirror appears laterally inverted.
questions.answer: In a plane mirror, the left and right sides of the object appear reversed due to reflection of light.

questions.question: Why does a concave mirror produce a magnified image when the object is close?
questions.answer: When the object is placed between the mirror and the focal point, the reflected rays form a larger virtual image.

questions.question: Explain how periscopes help submarines observe objects above water.
questions.answer: Periscopes use two mirrors placed at angles to reflect light from objects above water into the observer’s eyes.

questions.question: Why do objects become invisible in darkness?
questions.answer: Objects are invisible in darkness because no light reflects from them into our eyes.

questions.question: Explain why the brightness of an image depends on reflected light.
questions.answer: The more light reflected from an object or mirror, the brighter the image appears.

questions.question: Why does a glass lens bend light?
questions.answer: A glass lens bends light because light changes speed when passing from one medium to another, causing refraction.

questions.question: Explain why the image formed by a plane mirror cannot be projected on a screen.
questions.answer: The image formed by a plane mirror is virtual and cannot be projected on a screen because light rays do not actually meet.

questions.question: Why do convex lenses focus sunlight to a point?
questions.answer: Convex lenses converge parallel rays of sunlight to a focal point.

questions.question: Explain why concave lenses are used in spectacles for short-sightedness.
questions.answer: Concave lenses spread light rays so that the image forms correctly on the retina.

questions.question: Why do security mirrors in shops use convex mirrors?
questions.answer: Convex mirrors allow a wider viewing area, helping monitor larger spaces.

questions.question: Explain why concave mirrors are used in headlights.
questions.answer: Concave mirrors reflect light forward in a strong beam, improving visibility.

questions.question: Why do lenses form images?
questions.answer: Lenses form images because they bend light rays through refraction.

questions.question: Explain why studying mirrors and lenses is important in science.
questions.answer: Understanding mirrors and lenses helps develop optical instruments such as cameras, microscopes, and telescopes.
